These elections will fill vacant legislative seats, ensuring full representation in state assemblies.]]></description><link><![CDATA[https://timesofindia.indiatimes.com/india/ec-announces-bypoll-dates-for-8-assembly-constituencies-across-six-states-check-schedule/articleshow/129589440.cms]]></link><guid><![CDATA[https://timesofindia.indiatimes.com/india/ec-announces-bypoll-dates-for-8-assembly-constituencies-across-six-states-check-schedule/articleshow/129589440.cms]]></guid><pubDate>Sun, 15 Mar 2026 17:37:23 +0530</pubDate><dc:creator>TOI NEWS DESK</dc:creator><enclosure type="image/jpeg" length="404755" url="https://static.toiimg.com/photo/msid-129589437,imgsize-404755.cms"/></item><item><title><![CDATA[Mamata raises monthly honorarium for priests, muezzins ahead of poll schedule announcement]]></title><description><![CDATA[West Bengal Chief Minister Mamata Banerjee announced a Rs 500 hike in monthly honorariums for priests and muezzins, raising their pay to Rs 2,000. Banerjee stated the decision recognizes the vital role of religious functionaries in community life.]]></description><link><![CDATA[https://timesofindia.indiatimes.com/india/mamata-banerjee-raises-monthly-honorarium-for-priests-muezzins-by-rs-500-ahead-of-ecs-poll-schedule-announcement/articleshow/129587805.cms]]></link><guid><![CDATA[https://timesofindia.indiatimes.com/india/mamata-banerjee-raises-monthly-honorarium-for-priests-muezzins-by-rs-500-ahead-of-ecs-poll-schedule-announcement/articleshow/129587805.cms]]></guid><pubDate>Sun, 15 Mar 2026 15:39:35 +0530</pubDate><dc:creator>TOI NEWS DESK</dc:creator><enclosure type="image/jpeg" length="682612" url="https://static.toiimg.com/photo/msid-129587852,imgsize-682612.cms"/></item><item><title><![CDATA[Mother quit job, son scripts history: How Mayank, 16, became Northeast’s 1st GM]]></title><description><![CDATA[Sixteen-year-old Mayank Chakraborty has made history as the first Grandmaster from Assam and Northeast India.     <item>
      <title><![CDATA[Energy crunch disrupts weddings, restaurants and gig workers: Industry bodies]]></title>
      <link><![CDATA[https://www.cnbctv18.com/videos/business/gas-shortage-impact-weddings-restaurants-gig-workers-india-zomato-swiggy-19867258.htm]]></link>
      <description><![CDATA[Energy supply disruptions linked to tensions around the Strait of Hormuz are beginning to hit businesses on the ground, with industry groups flagging problems during the peak wedding season. Sukhdev Singh Sidhu, President of the Punjab Marriage Palace & Resort Association, said gas shortages have disrupted catering operations at wedding venues, making it difficult to cook and serve food smoothly at large events. Energy expert Narendra Taneja added that tanker movement through the Strait remains uncertain amid heightened security risks. The impact is also spreading to the gig economy. Shaik Salauddin, General Secretary of the Indian Federation of App-Based Transport Workers, said CNG shortages and rising fuel costs are squeezing driver incomes and forcing some gas-dependent restaurants to temporarily shut operations.]]></description>
      <pubDate><![CDATA[Thu, 12 Mar 2026 19:03:10 +0530]]></pubDate>
      <dc:creator>Mitali Mohite</dc:creator>
      <guid><![CDATA[https://www.cnbctv18.com/videos/business/gas-shortage-impact-weddings-restaurants-gig-workers-india-zomato-swiggy-19867258.htm]]></guid>

    </item>

      <title><![CDATA[LPG crunch shuts 20% restaurants; industry urges govt to prioritise commercial supply]]></title>

      <description><![CDATA[India’s restaurant industry is facing a major disruption due to an acute shortage of commercial LPG cylinders. Vijay Shetty President of Indian Hotel & Restaurant Association (AHAR) said around 20% of restaurants have already shut and more could close within days if supply does not improve. NRAI Vice President Zorawar Kalra warned that nearly 75% of restaurants depend on LPG, making the crisis severe for both organised and small eateries. Industry bodies are urging the government to prioritise commercial LPG supply and consider restaurants as essential services.]]></description>

      <title><![CDATA[Aim for $1 trillion trade, not $500 billion, says USIBC President Atul Keshap]]></title>

      <description><![CDATA[Atul Keshap, President of the US-India Business Council (USIBC), has called for a far more ambitious economic partnership between India and the United States, suggesting that the bilateral trade target should move beyond $500 billion and aim for $1 trillion.       <description><![CDATA[Polymer prices have risen sharply by 45–50% within a week, creating pressure on India’s plastic manufacturing sector, according to Sunil Shah, President of All India Plastics Manufacturers Association. With nearly 80–90% of the industry made up of MSMEs, the sudden spike linked to higher crude and feedstock costs is difficult for smaller firms to absorb. Manufacturers say frequent price hikes, supply uncertainty and limited inventory capacity could disrupt production and margins across the sector.]]></description>

      <title><![CDATA[India’s exports may take $7–8 billion hit if West Asia conflict persists: FIEO]]></title>
      <link><![CDATA[https://www.cnbctv18.com/videos/business/west-asia-conflict-hits-indian-exporters-freight-costs-jump-up-to-300-pc-says-fieo-19864807.htm]]></link>
      <description><![CDATA[India’s exporters are facing growing pressure as the West Asia conflict disrupts shipping routes and drives up freight and insurance costs. According to FIEO Director General Ajay Sahai, export orders are getting delayed and containers are stuck at ports, affecting exporters’ cash flows. Freight rates have surged sharply, with some routes seeing costs nearly triple, while air freight has more than doubled. If the situation continues through March, India’s exports could take a hit of $7–8 billion.]]></description>
      <pubDate><![CDATA[Mon, 9 Mar 2026 15:08:17 +0530]]></pubDate>

      <title><![CDATA[Freight surge, port delays hit rice exports; 4 lakh tonne of basmati at risk: Exporters Federation]]></title>

      <description><![CDATA[Indian rice exports are facing disruption as rising marine fuel costs, higher freight rates and shipping delays impact shipments, according to Dev Garg of the Indian Rice Exporters Federation. Around 3,000 containers of basmati rice are stuck at ports such as Mundra and Kandla, while large quantities remain in transit or at destination ports. With freight costs rising sharply and insurance premiums increasing, exporters warn that nearly 3.5–4 lakh tonne of basmati rice shipments are currently at risk.]]></description>
      <pubDate><![CDATA[Mon, 9 Mar 2026 14:42:14 +0530]]></pubDate>

      <description><![CDATA[When India's largest oil explorer opened a tender for a cement order in 2018, it sensed something was off by the competing bids coming in: all of them were exactly 7,000 rupees per tonne.]]></description>
      <pubDate><![CDATA[Mon, 9 Mar 2026 14:36:49 +0530]]></pubDate>

    <item>
      <title><![CDATA[Watch | Premiumisation, category creation key to next phase of FMCG growth: HUL’s Priya Nair]]></title>
      <link><![CDATA[https://www.cnbctv18.com/videos/business/hul-premiumisation-category-creation-fmcg-growth-priya-nair-19863816.htm]]></link>
      <description><![CDATA[Premiumisation and the creation of new product categories will be key drivers of the next phase of growth for the fast-moving consumer goods (FMCG) sector in India, according to Priya Nair, CEO of Hindustan Unilever.Speaking in an interview to CNBC-TV18, Nair said India’s consumption story is closely intertwined with the growth trajectory of Hindustan Unilever, as the country enters what she described as an important economic inflection point.“We cannot tell the story of HUL without telling the story of India because India and HUL are strongly linked,” Nair said, adding that India remains a bright spot globally at a time when several major economies face slower growth.She pointed out that India’s per capita consumption of consumer goods remains significantly lower than that of several Southeast Asian economies, indicating significant room for expansion in the years ahead.“At $54 per capita, it is significantly lower than many neighbouring peers… so there is huge headroom to grow,” she said.For Hindustan Unilever, the opportunity lies in expanding consumption by developing new categories and strengthening brands. Nair said the company has historically focused on building categories and would continue to do so as consumer behaviour evolves.“The biggest opportunity lies in growing and creating categories. That’s what we have done, and that’s what we will continue to do,” she said.While the fundamental approach to brand building remains unchanged, the ways in which companies engage consumers are rapidly evolving, driven by shifts in media consumption and distribution channels.Nair also highlighted India’s favourable demographics, noting that the country now has nearly 400 million Gen Z consumers whose preferences are reshaping consumption patterns.“India today is more ready than ever before,” she said, citing rising incomes, digital acceleration and changing consumer aspirations as key forces driving demand.As a result, she believes India’s consumption story is still in its early stages, presenting a significant opportunity for companies that can innovate, premiumise and build relevant brands for the next generation of consumers.]]></description>
      <pubDate><![CDATA[Fri, 6 Mar 2026 19:57:41 +0530]]></pubDate>
      <dc:creator>Swapnil Deshpande</dc:creator>
      <guid><![CDATA[https://www.cnbctv18.com/videos/business/hul-premiumisation-category-creation-fmcg-growth-priya-nair-19863816.htm]]></guid>
      <category><![CDATA[Business]]></category>
      <media:content height="675" width="1200" medium="image" url="https://images.cnbctv18.com/uploads/2026/03/1772807264_hKvpiBwGgH-hul-priya-nair-06marjpg-z1usYCUOAo.jpg"/>
    </item>
